About.title: Mgr. Daniel Budziňák
About.bio: Product manager connecting business, data, and intelligent workflows — technology should give people time back, not take it.

intro.lead: My journey did not start with AI. Over the last 20 years I have worked across every key layer of a digital product:
intro.b1: Graphic design and visual communication: I know what makes design feel premium and functional.
intro.b2: Marketing and UX/UI: I understand user psychology and how data shifts conversions.
intro.b3: Project and product management: I know how to steer priorities, budgets, and team energy.
intro.closing: Today I connect those isolated worlds into one whole. I am a product manager who believes technology should give people time back, not take it. I help companies make better decisions through data and intelligent workflows.

AboutPage.philosophyText: I do not chase the most complicated solution — I look for the one that delivers the greatest impact right now. My work is about aligning business needs with today’s technological reality.
AboutPage.valueText: Because I have walked the full path from the first pixel to strategic leadership, I can today use AI tools (such as Cursor and Claude) to validate ideas and build systems at a pace that was impossible years ago. I focus on systems thinking that lets companies grow fast without unnecessary technical debt.

t1.desc: Development of an AI-driven decision intelligence system that transforms marketing and UX data into structured workflows.

t2.desc: Implementation of AI-assisted workflows and automation systems for digital projects.

t3.desc: Management of complex web application and e-commerce solution delivery.

t4.desc: Development of one of the first boxed e-commerce systems on the Slovak market.

p1.title: Travel
p1.desc: I enjoy discovering new cultures and cuisine, with Italy and Spain as recurring destinations.

p2.title: Music
p2.desc: It supports both my work and recovery rhythm and helps me keep high focus.

p3.title: Family
p3.desc: My free time belongs to family moments and work in the garden.

Shopentum.heroTitle: High data volume. Low decision clarity.
Shopentum.heroLead: E-commerce teams process fragmented data across systems without clear inference. Shopentum introduces a deterministic decision layer.
Shopentum.diagramTitle: Shopentum connects data and turns it into clear decisions.
Shopentum.diagramLead: By connecting marketing and technical signals, we identify the most critical growth barriers in e-commerce operations.
Shopentum.engineLead: Stores context, evaluates audit data, GA4, and Google/Meta Ads signals in real time, then drives execution based on market reality and business goals.
Shopentum.problemTitle: The store operated, but decision logic was weak.

problems.i2: Marketing and data were not connected (data silos)
problems.i3: Optimization was slow, reactive, and expensive
problems.i4: The system did not scale without constant team growth

Shopentum.architectureLead: I designed a custom decision layer architecture built on AI WORKS principles.

c1.title: Runtime vs Snapshot
c1.desc: Strict separation between real-time processing and immutable historical states for fully auditable decisions.

c2.title: Pipeline-based Processing
c2.desc: No ad-hoc scripts. Chained data pipeline from raw collection to strategic insight.

c3.title: Delta Engine
c3.desc: Continuously compares current state against benchmarks and explains cause and business impact over time.

c4.title: Findings Engine
c4.desc: Multi-layer analytics that detects critical barriers and growth opportunities in data noise.

log.l2: [DATA] Fetching snapshots from GA4 & Meta API
log.l3: [ENGINE] Delta analysis complete: +14% deviation in ROAS

solutionList.i1: A system that tracks marketing state over time

m4.label: Scaling
m4.desc: No need to constantly increase team size.

Shopentum.ctaTitle: Need a technical architect for your AI initiative?
Shopentum.ctaLead: Define the challenge. I will design the logic layer and deploy a system that turns data into decisions.

AIArchitecture.heroLead: I design and implement decision intelligence systems that are secure, auditable, and fully embedded in your business processes.
AIArchitecture.quote: Twenty years in digital taught me the biggest risk is not technology — it is chaos. My AI architecture brings order and business logic to generative workflows. I do not sell prompts; I sell infrastructure for how you decide.
AIArchitecture.diagramLead: Example of a state-driven architecture where agents activate dynamically based on the current state of the object.

governance.title: Governance, Security & Control Layer
governance.sub: Policy • Risk • Human Authority • Publish Gate

identity.title: Brand Identity Layer
identity.desc: Every brand has its own tone, rules, and risk profile. (Multi-brand / multi-tenant architecture).

core.title: Common Intelligence Core
core.sub: Shared Memory • Audit Trail • Decision Context • Content • Claims • Policy • Events

b1.title: Security & compliance
b1.desc: Full control over data and outputs (governance). Your know-how never leaves a secure perimeter.

b2.title: Scalability
b2.desc: Multi-tenant architecture supports one system for multiple brands, markets, or departments while preserving identity.

b3.title: Auditability
b3.desc: Every AI decision is recorded in an audit trail and verifiable afterward. No more black box.

b4.title: Efficiency
b4.desc: Automation of routine analytical and creative work frees senior experts for strategic work.

cta.title: AI architecture under control
cta.lead: I will design a closed Intelligence Engine that respects your identity and security rules — architecture that keeps your business governable.

IzyvapeMvp.heroLead: We build a layer that connects your existing systems via APIs, evaluates inventory signals and produces concrete recommended tasks for the team on a recurring basis. Not another dashboard or a chat bolt-on.

diagram.title: Connected data and APIs with a decision outcome
diagram.lead: Same pattern as multi-source decision intelligence: multiple inputs, one normalization and evaluation step, outputs your team can schedule as work.
diagram.engineLead: Normalizes the product across systems, keeps time-based snapshots, surfaces findings (risks and opportunities) and turns them into prioritized operational recommendations.

essence.title: From system wiring to operational tasks
essence.lead: A shared product model, recurring data snapshots and a decision layer. The outcome is not only a chart but an actionable sequence of steps.

s1.title: Data and API connectivity
s1.body: Read from systems you already run. One canonical product entity across WooCommerce, Money S, Metorik and B2B APIs – without rebuilding your stack.

s2.title: Decision layer over time
s2.body: Rules and findings over historical snapshots: stockout risk, trapped capital, anomalies. Humans decide and execute in the original tools.

s3.title: Tasks instead of widgets
s3.body: Each finding maps to work: what is happening, why it matters, suggested next step and priority. No autonomous stock or price changes from this layer.

snapshot.title: Snapshots for comparison across periods
snapshot.lead: Live operational state and an immutable cut at a point in time are separate concepts. Snapshots let you compare yesterday, last week or any interval – instead of interpreting one instantaneous figure without context.
snapshot.b1: Day-over-day and week-over-week views using the same calculation logic.
snapshot.b2: Deviation and exception detection inside the chosen window.
snapshot.b3: Auditability: tie a finding to state-at-time and rule version.
snapshot.b4: Foundation for trends and seasonality without losing continuity.

IzyvapeMvp.stackFootnote: Same delivery stack used for other production systems on this site.
IzyvapeMvp.flowsTitle: Three areas in the first scope
IzyvapeMvp.flowsLead: A narrow slice for fast validation against warehouse and sales reality; broader scope after data stabilizes.

f1.title: Stockout risk
f1.question: What is most likely to sell through before replenishment arrives?
f1.output: Urgency, horizon estimate, confidence and a proposed next step from available signals (velocity, reservations, etc.).

f2.title: Dead stock / Overstock
f2.question: Which items trap capital or barely move?
f2.output: Ranked candidates for markdown, promo or other actions – tied to inventory value and movement.

f3.title: Expiration risk
f3.question: What could expire before it clears?
f3.output: Time priority and recommended handling – only with trustworthy expiry data; otherwise explicit DATA GAP.

IzyvapeMvp.accordionLead: Optional reading after the main narrative.

gov.title: Governance and principles
gov.i1: System proposes and prioritizes; people execute in existing tools.
gov.i2: Missing data is not padded with guesses – explicit DATA GAP.
gov.i3: Descriptive and comparative logic first; heavier prediction and automation once the dataset is stable.
gov.i4: Stable snapshot structure and repeatability before demanding perfect field completeness.
gov.i5: No misleading hype – measurable findings and defendable rules.

scope.title: Outside the first MVP
scope.i1: A conversational bot as the primary product value.
scope.i2: Autonomous price, campaign or stock changes without approval.
scope.i3: Full marketing intelligence in the same commitment as stock – planned later.
scope.i5: Write-back to WooCommerce or ERP in phase one.
scope.i6: Opaque LLM outputs without rule and snapshot grounding.

metrics.title: Validation
metrics.i1: Counts and lists reconciled against a sampled SKU review.
metrics.i2: Dead-stock outputs consistent with inventory or an approved subset.
metrics.i3: Short leadership summary: critical items and actions needed this week.
metrics.i4: Whether tasks from the product actually move decisions – not raw click counts.

deps.title: Dependencies and discovery
deps.i1: API access and agreement on source of truth for stock.
deps.i2: Business parameters: lead times, owners, meaning of reservations.
deps.i3: Short discovery before a binding schedule.
deps.i4: Coordination with your developer when connecting services.

phase2.title: Phase 2 (indicative)
phase2.body: Marketing signals, richer alerting and further use cases after the stock decision layer runs reliably and earns team trust.

IzyvapeMvp.docFootnote: Full markdown spec (MVP + Jira-ready block) lives in the project repo or I can send it on request.
IzyvapeMvp.closingTitle: From concept to a concrete plan
IzyvapeMvp.closingLead: Short discovery, then a binding plan for ingestion, first snapshots and findings v1 – without reorganizing your entire systems stack.
